mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate trend over the trailing 6 months increases the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and raises the cost of leverage, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the tightening trend persists.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 6-month trend and 20-day forward SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive returns occur in >55% of months with rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE ticks up by at least 0.2 percentage points from its trailing 3-month low, equity markets price in an imminent growth deceleration, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as risk premia reprice quickly around the labor print.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6%, near cycle lows in mid-2006
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, mean 5-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) or hit rate of negative returns falls below 50% over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in HOUST and deceleration in RSAFS growth signals a broad-based consumer and housing demand slowdown that compounds over the following quarter, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days because neither series alone captures the combined demand shock magnitude.
candidate_trigger: HOUST down >5% YoY and RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1849k, down from 2005 peaks near 2200k
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show 60-day forward SPX returns not significantly more negative than unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or Rank IC of interaction term below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion with tightening monetary policy, positive INDPRO growth reflects durable production strength that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; this relationship is expected to be materially weaker or reversed outside late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth periods within the regime fail to produce 20-day forward returns exceeding the unconditional mean by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10) across two independent late-cycle episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Once FEDFUNDS crosses above 5%, the policy rate approaches or exceeds nominal GDP growth capacity, historically preceding credit tightening and equity market stress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.94%, approaching the 5% threshold in mid-2006
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle nearing terminal rate
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following FEDFUNDS > 5% crossings are not significantly negative (p > 0.10), or fewer than 50% of such episodes show negative direction over the historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS above its trailing 6-month average signals resilient labor income and consumption capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets extrapolate continued earnings support.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135106k continuing modest monthly gains in 2006
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day forward SPX return stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or hit rate of positive returns falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where housing starts (HOUST) have declined more than 15% from their cyclical peak while the Fed funds rate remains elevated, the housing downturn signals an impending broader demand contraction that compounds over a quarter, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; outside this regime the HOUST-return link is expected to be weak or absent.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1849k, down from prior peak near 2273k in early 2005
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing downturn and elevated policy rates
failure_condition: Within the regime, 60-day forward SPX returns following HOUST declines >15% from peak are not significantly negative (p > 0.10) across at least two historical regime episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains elevated above 3.5% while FEDFUNDS simultaneously exceeds 4.5%, markets price stagflation risk where tightening cannot be paused despite slowing growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; this interaction is not captured by either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 201.9 with FEDFUNDS at 4.94% in mid-2006
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to produce significantly negative 20-day forward SPX returns relative to baseline (p > 0.10), or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
